name: Appenzeller Sennenhund
description: The Appenzeller Sennenhund is one of Switzerland's four Sennenhund breeds, originating in the Appenzell region of Switzerland where it served as a versatile farm dog herding cattle, guarding property, and pulling carts. This lively, self-confident breed is the least commonly seen of the four Swiss mountain dogs internationally, despite its excellent all-around abilities and striking tricolored appearance. Unlike the other Swiss mountain dogs, the Appenzeller carries its thick-boned tail tightly curled over its back in typical Spitz fashion, giving it a distinctive silhouette.

conditions_detail.name: Hip Dysplasia
conditions_detail.description: Hip dysplasia is a genetic condition where the hip joint doesn't develop properly, causing the ball and socket to not fit together correctly. This leads to joint deterioration, pain, and arthritis over time.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Treatment ranges from weight management and physical therapy for mild cases to total hip replacement for severe cases. NSAIDs and joint supplements help manage pain.
prevention_tips: Screen breeding stock with hip scoring
